encounter.type: Office visit, established patient, results review and treatment initiation
encounter.reason: Atrial fibrillation results review, medication initiation, electrophysiology referral
encounter.location: Minnehaha Internal Medicine, 3501 East Lake Street, Minneapolis, MN 55406

conditions.name: atrial fibrillation, new-onset
conditions.note: Workup reviewed confirming diagnosis: ECG shows AF rate 92, echo shows preserved EF 58%, mild LA dilation 4.3 cm, no valvular disease, labs normal, no reversible cause. CHA2DS2-VASc score 1. Treatment initiated with rate control and anticoagulation. Now further characterized as new-onset AF with preserved cardiac function, mild left atrial dilation, and no identified reversible etiology.

medications.name: metoprolol succinate 50 mg extended-release tablet
medications.sig: Take one 50 mg extended-release tablet by mouth once daily for ventricular rate control

medications.name: apixaban 5 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take one 5 mg tablet by mouth twice daily for stroke prevention
medications.reason: atrial fibrillation (anticoagulation for stroke prevention); dose based on age <80, weight >60 kg, creatinine <1.5

document_references.description: Detailed office visit note documenting comprehensive review of atrial fibrillation workup results (ECG, echocardiogram, labs), CHA2DS2-VASc score calculation (score of 1), shared decision-making discussion regarding anticoagulation with patient and wife, initiation of metoprolol succinate for rate control and apixaban for stroke prevention, counseling on medication side effects and precautions, and referral to cardiac electrophysiology for catheter ablation evaluation. 40-minute visit with greater than 50% in counseling and coordination of care.

diagnostic_reports.description: Atrial fibrillation, ventricular rate 92 bpm, normal axis, no ST-segment changes. Reviewed at this visit.
Unremarkable — hemoglobin 14.8 g/dL, WBC 6.2 × 10³/µL, platelets 234 × 10³/µL. Reviewed at this visit.
Normal — creatinine 0.94 mg/dL, potassium 4.1 mEq/L, glucose 98 mg/dL. Reviewed at this visit.
TSH 2.1 mIU/L, normal. No thyroid disease identified. Reviewed at this visit.
Mild left atrial dilation (4.3 cm), preserved left ventricular ejection fraction 58%, no significant valvular disease, no pericardial effusion, estimated RVSP normal. Reviewed at this visit.
